title: Geraniospermum adulterinum
taxonomy: Geraniospermum adulterinum is a species of flowering plant in the family Menispermaceae. It belongs to the genus Geraniospermum, which is a group of plants commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The family Menispermaceae is a group of flowering plants that are often referred to as moonseed plants due to the shape of their seeds. The classification of Geraniospermum adulterinum places it within the larger group of angiosperms, or flowering plants. This species is likely to be further classified at the species level using a binomial nomenclature system.

Geranium (Geraniospermum or Geranium spp.) is a genus of flowering plants in the family Geraniaceae, commonly known for their fragrant flowers and medicinal properties. The health benefits of Geranium plants include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may help to soothe skin irritations, reduce stress, and promote wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Geranium plants include flavonoids, tannins, and essential oils, particularly geraniol and linalool, which are responsible for their therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Geranium plants include teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which are used to treat various health conditions, including anxiety, insomnia, and skin issues.
